trg: xabra m-mani hawex? xabra hawex m-xa ň§dae ň§qari.
eng: From whom do we have a word? We have a word from an old woman.

trg: belaw ň§qrańü ň§ahra wele
eng: Her house was in the outskirts of the country

trg: xa brona …ôtwala, ň§r…ôwwa brona
eng: She had one son, a grown boy

trg: ya brona heńć la-godwa: xa Ň°rae nixe brona wele
eng: This boy did nothing: he was a "lamps extinguished" boy

trg: kulla yoma yat…ôwwa reŇ° tanura. kańćal reŇ°ew m-qardew gal miye hammeŇ°a k…ôsya wele ki kańćalew kasela
eng: Every day, he would sit upon the tanura (oven). His bald head was always covered from the cold with "miye" so that he covers his baldness

trg: m…ôn-d-o yoma Ň°…ômmew xd…ôre kańćalona.
eng: From (since) that day, his name became "kańćalona" (baldie)

trg: k…ômm…ôt ň§dae ň§qari maqyawa gallew, taqya …ôllew, lawya …ôllew: "ň§qurbanox xadran, si xa Ň°ula wud, kulla yoma ytiwet reŇ° tanura, heńć m…ôndińü la-wadet"
eng: How much the old woman would speak with him! She pleads with him and begs him: "may I be your sacrifice, go do one job, All day you have sat upon the oven, you are doing nothing"

trg: kańćalona m…ôre ň§ba-daew: "ana m…ôn-d-ay tanura b-la-koŇ°en, qardi ila, pruq m…ônni. abulat ň§noxoŇ° xadren"
eng: kańćalona said to his mother: "I will not come down from this tanura. I am cold, leave me be (finish from me). You want me to become sick."

trg: agar tara palxawale, ň§nare dah…ôlwa, km…ôrwa: "ň§dae ň§qari, tara m…ôqwile, la-god poxa taqra …ôlli, ň§noxoŇ° xadren. tara tez m…ôqwile"
eng: If she would open the door, he would make a clamour. He would say: "Old woman, close the door so it won't make wind touch me and make me sick. Close the door quickly"

trg: ň§daew k…ômrawa: "ň§qurbanox xadran, reŇ° tanura ytiwet, m-Ň°…ôxna qelet, la qablet tara palx…ôxxe, ň§axr ana m…ôlli, ma bodan gallox? ň§plut si tara gal jwanqe, xdur lagab logab. balki xa Ň°ula ň§maŇ°txet, balki goret."
eng: His mother would reply: "May I beome your sacrifice, you have sat upon the tanura, you shall burn from the heat! You don't allow us to open the door. At last I'm dead, what will I do with you? Come out, go outside with the young men, wander hither and thither. Perhaps you could find a job, perhaps you could get married."

trg: kud yom b-yoma haman masala wela. ň§dae ň§qari maqyawa, kańćalona reŇ° tanura yat…ôwwa
eng: Every day by day it was the same story. The old woman would speak, kańćalona would sit upon the tanura

trg: xabra m-mani hawex? xabra hawex ň§m-Ň°ultan…ôt ň§ahra
eng: From whom do we have a word? We have a word from the king of the country

trg: ň§Ň°ultana p…ôlle ň§noxoŇ°.

trg: ň§r…ôqqa m-amrane, ň§w-r…ôqqa m-Ň°amyane, enew ň§kor xd…ôru
eng: Far be it from the teller, and far be it from the listener, his eyes became blind

trg: wazirew p…ôllu balbole ba-darmane ba ň§Ň°ultana.
eng: His wazirs (advisors) fell searching for medicine for the king

trg: lek…ôt hakima itwa, mdelule geb ň§Ň°ultana.
eng: Wherever there was a doctor, they brought him to the king's house.

trg: xabra hw…ôllu g-kullu ň§ahra mani-t ň§mse darmana mde ň§ba-Ň°ultana ki enew palxi, mar mde.
eng: The gave out a statement in the whole city: "whoever can bring medicine to the king that [can make] his eyes open, let them bring"

trg: k…ômm…ôt darmane ň§jr…ôblu, enew la pl…ôxlu.
eng: However many medicines they tried, his eyes didn't open.

trg: ň§Ň°ultana m…ôre ba-wazirew: "bai tez ň§ńćara ň§m…ôŇ°tuxun"
eng: The king said to his wazirs: "Find a remedy quickly for me"

trg: …ôdyelu naŇ°e geb ň§Ň°ultana m…ôru baew: "ň§ańüaan ň§Ň°ultana, mani ň§mse baox ň§ńćara od? xa danka d…ôqna-xwara ita ytiwele g-xa ň§mańüara ň§r…ôqqa ň§m-ahra. o balki ň§ńćarox godla"
eng: People came to the house of the king. They said to him: "Our lord, the king, who can make a remedy for you? There is a certain d…ôqna-xwara (old man) who is sat in a cave far from the city. Perhaps he can make your remedy"

trg: ň§Ň°ultana xabra hw…ôlle ba-wazirew: "tez simun …ôl-d-o d…ôqna-xwara, mdimune laxxa"
eng: The king gave a statement to his wazirs: "You all go quickly to this d…ôqna-xwara! Bring him here"

trg: wazire tez ň§pl…ôtlu, xabr…ôt ň§Ň°ultana odile.
eng: The wazirs went out quickly to fulfill the king's statement.

trg: …ôl d…ôqna-xwara ň§m-mańüara ň§pl…ôtlule, mdelule ň§qanŇ°ar ň§Ň°ultana¬†
eng: They brought out the d…ôqna-xwara from the cavern, they brought him before the king.¬†

trg: d…ôqna-xwara, d…ôqnew xwarta yar…ôxta. ň§salmew mare ň§hesta wele.
eng: The d…ôqna-xwara, his beard was white and long. His face is saying it was honored (his face shows honor).

trg: reŇ°ew mk…ôplele ň§qam ň§Ň°ultana
eng: he bowed his head in front of the king

trg: x…ôre …ôllew s…ônńći m…ôndele …ôllew, m…ôre baew: "ň§ańüai ň§Ň°ultana, Ň°ulox ň§raba yaqura ile, ň§ńćarox ň§raba yaqurtela"
eng: he saw him, he cast his attention to him (thoroughly examined), he said to him: "my lord the king, your business is very heavy (situation is grave), your remedy is very heavy (will take a lot of work)"

trg: ň§Ň°ultana m…ôre baew: "hala eni la-x…ôzyettu, ma-to ayolet ma-tfiq …ôl-eni?"
eng: The king said to him: "you haven't seen my eyes yet, how are you knowing what has happened to my eyes?"

trg: m…ôre baew: "ň§Ň°ultana, ň§qurbanox xadren, …ôllox s…ônńći m…ôndeli, dardox y…ôllile, ň§ńćarox…ôŇ° ky…ônna"
eng: He said to him: "ň§Ň°ultana, may I become your sacrifice, I cast my attention to you (examined you thoroughly), I know your illness, (and) I know your remedy as well"

trg: ň§Ň°ultana, wazirew, wakilew, kullu mahtal p…ôŇ°lu
eng: the king, his wazirs, his ministers -- everyone -- fell astonished

trg: d…ôqna-xwara m…ôre ň§ba-Ň°ultana: "ň§laz…ôm ile baox madeni xalw…ôt ň§aslan g-m…ôŇ°k…ôt ň§aslan reŇ° ň§xas…ôt ň§aslan"
eng: The d…ôqna-xwara said to the king: "It is necessary for you [that] they bring milk of a lion in the skin of a lion on the back of a lion"

trg: "o xalwa dar…ôtte reŇ° enox, enox palxi"
eng: Should you put that milk upon your eyes, your eyes shall open

trg: wazire wakile kullu mahtal p…ôŇ°lu. ň§lal xd…ôrru.
eng: The wazirs and the ministers all fell astonished. They went silent.

trg: ň§Ň°ultana m…ôre ba-wazirew ba-wakilew: "ya Ň°ula mani ň§mse odle?"
eng: The king said to his wazirs and his ministers: "Who can do this job?"

trg: wazire wakile ň§lal p…ôŇ°lu, naŇ° m-tkew ň§la-b…ôrb…ôtle. m…ôru baew: "ň§Ň°ultana ň§qurbanox xadrex, mani ň§mer ya Ň°ula odle? xalw…ôt ň§aslan g-m…ôŇ°k…ôt ň§aslan reŇ° ň§xas…ôt ň§aslan"
eng: The wazirs and the ministers fell silent. Nobody moved from their place. They said to them: ň§Ň°ultana, may we become your sacrifice, who could dare to do this job? Milk of a lion in the skin of a lion on the back of a lion."

trg: ň§Ň°ultana m…ôre: "tez xabra holun g-…ôprit ň§Ň°ultanuli g-kullu mamlakate, mani ile gora gabbara zudda, ez…ôl darmana made ň§ba-Ň°ultana -- xalw…ôt ň§aslan g-m…ôŇ°k…ôt ň§aslan reŇ° ň§xas…ôt ň§aslan -- mani-t xabri odle x…ôtn…ôt ň§Ň°ultana xad…ôr, ň§sańü idi yat…ôw"
eng: The king said: "Give out a statement quickly within the soil of my kingdom, in every state. Whoever is a brave hero should go bring medicine to the king -- milk of a lion in the skin of a lion on the back of a lion -- whoever can do my command will become the son in law of the king, he shall sit at my right hand"

trg: ya xabra ň§mtele …ôl-naŇ°e …ôl-maxluqat, kud naŇ° …ôbele x…ôtnet ň§Ň°ultana xad…ôr
eng: This statement arrived to the people and creations. Everybody wanted to become the king's son-in-law!

trg: ň§Ň°ultana ň§raba kaŇ°iŇ° wele. mani-t ya Ň°ula odle, tk…ôt ň§Ň°ultana xad…ôr. ň§tanjew darela reŇ°ew.
eng: the king was very withdrawn (monk-like). Whoever could do this job will take the king's place. He will put his crown upon him.

trg: kullu jwanqe kullu ň§gabbare ya xabra Ň°amyane geziwa darmana madoe, bale la-deriwa.
eng: All the young men, all the heroes; the hearers of this statement would go to bring the medicine, but they wouldn't return.

trg: ň§Ň°ultana p…ôŇ°le ň§kor
eng: The king fell blind.

trg: xabra m-mani hawex? xabra hawex ň§m-dae ň§qari.
eng: From whom do we have a word? We have a word from the old woman.

trg: ň§dae ň§qari …ôl-d-ay xabra Ň°melale, …ôdyela geb kańćalona bronaw, m…ôra baew: "mani-t darmana ň§ba-Ň°ultana made, x…ôtnew xad…ôr"
eng: The old woman heard this decree, she came in to the dwelling of kańćalona, her son. She said to him: "Whoever brings medicine to the king shall become his son-in-law"

trg: kańćalona m…ôre "la, la, qurbanax xadran ň§dae, ana m…ôn-d-ayne Ň°ule wale reŇ°i ň§la-pal…ôt. pruq m…ônni ytiwen reŇ° tanura, tara la-pluxle, mar qarda la-qepa …ôlli. ana Ň°…ômm…ôt ň§aslan Ň°amulen m-zdeli myalen. ana majur bezen xalw…ôt ň§aslan maden ň§ba-Ň°ultana"
eng: kańćalona said: "No, no, may I become your sacrifice mother. My head doesn't come out from these affairs. Leave me alone, I have sat upon the oven, don't open the door, let the cold not knock into me (don't make me catch a cold). [When] I hear the name of a lion, I am dying from fear. How will I go bring milk of a lion to the king?"

trg: k…ômm…ôt ň§dae ň§qari lwela tqela …ôllew, ň§aman, ň§qurbanox xadran, heńć xabra la smele.
eng: However much the old woman begged and pleaded with him, "aman (woe), may I become your sacrifice," He didn't hear a word.

trg: m…ôre baaw: "tawaqa waden m…ônnax, ya xabra m-reŇ°ax ň§plutle"
eng: He said to her: "I have an expectation for you, take out this decree from your head"

trg: o-xet yoma baqatyo(m), ň§dae ň§qari aqlaw …ônt…ôyila, z…ôlla daŇ°ta, ň§siwe ň§q…ôrp…ôŇ°la ba-amblalu Ň°uqa, zabnalu, xanńći ixala Ň°aqla madya bela.
eng: In the morning the next day, the old woman picked up her feet, went to the field, gathered sticks [that] she will bring to the market to sell so that she might buy food to bring home.

trg: har ň§dae ň§qari ň§pl…ôtla m-bela, haman d…ôqna-xwara ň§m-mańüara tar…ôt ň§dae ň§qari pl…ôxlele.
eng: Just as the old woman went out from the house, the same d…ôqna-xwara from the cave opened the door of the old woman ['s house].

trg: ň§w…ôre l…ôwa. ň§sm…ôxle tuŇ°in kańćalona.
eng: He went inside. He stood opposite kańćalona.

trg: kańćalona reŇ°ew mr…ômlele xzele o-ile xa-danka d…ôqna-xwara ň§smix ň§qam tar…ôt bela.
eng: kańćalona raised his head, and he saw there is a d…ôqna-xwara who has stood in front of the the door to the house.

trg: x…ôre …ôllew, xzele d…ôqnew xwarta yar…ôxta, mare qoma, ň§salmew xwara, enew bahure, ň§m-salmew behra ň§palote.
eng: He looked at him and saw his long, white beard, a masterful stature, his light face and his bright eyes; a glow coming off of his face.

trg: kańćalona q…ômle mayrote, …ôdyele ň§qamew, d…ôqna nŇ°…ôqle. m…ôre: "gaw…ôt eni …ôdyelox, ň§matlubox ma-ile?"
eng: kańćalona rose trembling, he came before him and kissed his beard. He said: "You have come into my eyes, what is your request?"

trg: d…ôqna-xwara m…ôre: "ytuw tkox ana xa xabra bahw…ônnox"
eng: The d…ôqna-xwara said: "Sit in your place, I will give you one command"

trg: m…ôre baew d…ôqna-xwara: "darman…ôt ň§Ň°ultana har at gbe-mad…ôtte, ň§attot …ôdyo(m) Ň°w…ôqlilox reŇ° tanura ki ň§haz…ôr hawet ba-d-ay yoma"
eng: The d…ôqna-xwara said to him: "Just you must bring the king's medicine. Until today I kept you upon the oven so that you may be prepared for this day"

trg: "heńć ň§insan g-d-ay ň§ahra ň§aqlew ň§la-mte …ôllox, gena alpa Ň°…ônne. m-Ň°…ômme kl…ôwta wela, m-yom xadorox at gbe ň§Ň°ultan…ôt-d-ay ň§ahra xadret"
eng: No human in this country can reach your intelligence, even in a thousand years. From the Heavens, it was written on the day of your birth: you will become the king of this country."

trg: "yomox …ôdyo(m) mtele. ytuw ň§jwan Ň°mi ň§w…ôrxa mafkr…ônna baox ki ň§mset …ôl-d-ay Ň°ula ň§palt…ôtte"
eng: "Your day has come today. Sit, listen well, I shall explain to you the way that you can complete this job."

trg: kańćalona w…ôŇ°le, p…ôŇ°le g-tkew, mahtal p…ôŇ°le m…ôn-d-ayne xabre.
eng: kańćalona went stiff, he fell in his place, astonished at these words.

trg: m…ôre baew: "ň§ańüai, ma-t k…ômret, goden"
eng: He said to him: "my lord, whatever you will say, I will do"

trg: d…ôqna-xwara idew d…ôryile ň§g-b…ôlwanew, ň§pl…ôtle m…ônnew ň§taha m…ôsy…ôt susawe: xa m…ôsta xwarta, xa komta, xa smoqta.
eng: The d…ôqna-xwara put his hands into his pocket, and took out three horse hairs: one white hair, one black, and one red.

trg: m…ôre: "ayne m…ôsye drilu ň§g-b…ôrńüalox ň§paltet ezet ň§ba-w…ôrxox, iman…ôt xa-m…ôndińü taf…ôq …ôllox ň§g-w…ôrxa, xa m…ôsta ň§paltet maql…ôtta, suse gde ň§qamox gal kullu darzinew ň§xurjinew, sepa, badl…ôt rakkawe b-rang…ôt m…ôsta"
eng: He said: "Put these hairs in your inner jacket pocket, and set out and go on your way. Whenever something happens to you along the way, take out one hair and burn it. A horse shall come before you with all of these in the color of the hair: its harness, its saddlebag, a sword, and riding gear"

trg: "rakwet reŇ° suse, ň§paltet ň§ba-w…ôrxox, peret daŇ°te ň§w-turawe. ň§laz…ôm (ile) uŇ°wa ň§turawe per…ôttu, bar m…ôn-d-o xa naŇ° ň§pal…ôt ň§qamox maslahat kw…ôllox ki ň§w…ôrxox ň§tammam od…ôtta."
eng: You shall ride upon the horse, you shall set out on your way, you shall pass by fields and mountains. It is necessary that you pass by seven mountains, after that a person shall appear in front of you, and they will give you advice so that you might complete your journey.

trg: "baqatta baqatyo(m), bahurula gbe, ň§paltet ň§w…ôrxox"
eng: Tomorrow morning, when it is light, you must set out on your journey.

trg: xabra m-mani hawex? xabra hawex m-kańćalona
eng: From whom do we have a word? We have a word from kańćalona.

trg: axxona am…ôr baox, baqatyo(m) dunye bahore.
eng: a brother tells you, in the morning, the world brightens.

trg: kańćalona q…ômle xzele ň§dae ň§qari z…ôlla Ň°uqa.
eng: kańćalona arose, and he saw that the old woman went to the market.

trg: ň§jwan noŇ°ew ň§hamamlamiŇ° widale, ň§pl…ôtle ň§hayyat. s…ônńći m…ôndele ň§sańü gaba ň§sol gaba xzele naŇ° let
eng: He bathed himself well, and came out to the yard. He cast his attention to the left (side) and the right (side): there wasn't anybody.

trg: m…ôsta komta ň§p…ôltale ň§m-b…ôlwanew, m…ôqlale. xzele, b…ôrdan bira, xa ň§jwan suse, koma suse ň§sm…ôxle ň§qabańüew.
eng: He took out the black hair (strand) from his pocket and burned it. he saw, suddenly, one nice horse, a black horse, stood before him.

trg: mahtal p…ôŇ°le …ôl-d-ay suse. kullew koma, darzinew ň§xurjinew kullu kome ilu.
eng: He fell astonished at this horse. It was all black. Its harness and its saddlebag were all black.

trg: xzele reŇ°ew-…ôŇ° xa ň§jwan badla komta gal ksila komta, syame kome mabroqu magon ň§nohra.
eng: He also saw upon it a nice, black riding-suit with a black hat, and black shoes shining like a mirror.

trg: badla ma-badla, amret ň§m-mańüazy…ôt ň§Ň°ultane m…ôdyulla.
eng: The riding-suit: what a riding-suit! You might say they have brought it from the store of kings.

trg: tes tes badla lw…ôŇ°le ksila d…ôryale reŇ°ew, sepew reŇ° yanew ň§ys…ôrele.
eng: He quickly dressed into the riding-suit. He put the hat on his head, and he tied his sword on his side.

trg: x…ôre xzele ň§qab…ôt sepa ň§mańćroze ň§yaqute ň§jawah…ôrate. sepa sep…ôt ň§Ň°ultane.
eng: He looked and saw-- the sheath of the sword gleamed with sapphires and diamonds. The sword was a sword of kings.

trg: kańćalona reŇ° suse rk…ôwle, Ň°…ômmet …ôlhew mdelele, suse ň§m…ôtr…ôplele, p…ôlle ň§ba-w…ôrxew.
eng: kańćalona got on the horse, he brought the name of his Gd, he galloped the horse, and went on his way.

trg: xazule kańćalona ya suse k…ômma q…ôwyele, har amret ara xel aqlew ň§ryata.
eng: kańćalona sees how strong this horse is. Just as you might say, the ground under its feet is shaking.

trg: suse ň§farule magon poxa.
eng: The horse flies like the wind.

trg: belox la xar…ôw, m…ôre g-l…ôbbew ya d…ôqna-xwara kullu xabrew duz ilu.
eng: "your house shouldn't be destroyed," he said in his heart. "This d…ôqna-xwara, all of his words are true."

trg: x…ôre xzele ň§mtele ň§qanŇ°ar uŇ°wa ň§turawe
eng: he looked and saw that he arrived before seven mountains

trg: kańćalona gal susew ys…ôqle kw…ôŇ°le ys…ôqle kw…ôŇ°le. bar tre Ň°we, m…ôn-d-ayne ň§turawe kw…ôŇ°le.
eng: With his horse, kańćalona went up and down [the first mountain]. [Again and again], he went up and down. After two weeks, he came down from these mountains.

trg: b…ôrdan bira, dunye xŇ°…ôxla, la Ň°…ômŇ°a, la sehra, heńćm…ôndińü. x…ôŇ°ka x…ôŇ°k…ôt …ôlha.
eng: All of a sudden, the world darkened: no sun, no moon, nothing. The darkness was a darkness of God.

trg: b…ôrdan Ň°mele qal ň§xuyyawe, janaware, Ň°edime. zdela ň§w…ôra g-jandagew, p…ôlle aqle ide.
eng: Suddenly, he heard the voice of snakes, wild beasts, and demonic spirits (shedim). Fear entered his body. He fell to his hands and knees (lit. feet and hands)

trg: m…ôre g-l…ôbbew, "…ôlha ň§qurbanox xadren, leka p…ôlli, ma tf…ôqle …ôlli?"
eng: He said in his heart, "God -- may I be your sacrifice -- where I fall, what shall happen to me?"

trg: ň§attot o noŇ°ew ba-noŇ°ew maqule haman d…ôqna-xwara m-xa ň§mańüara ň§pl…ôtle ň§qamew
eng: While he is talking to himself, the same d…ôqna-xwara from a cave came out in front of him.

trg: m…ôre: "broni, lodet zadet, ya per…ôtte gal kulla ň§q…ôrtox"
eng: He said: "My boy, don't be afraid. You shall pass this with all your zeal."

trg: "ň§smux ň§qabańü d-ay ńćol ň§biyaban."
eng: "Stand before this deserted wilderness"

trg: "p…ônnox pluxle, qalox mrumle, amret ma ň§xoŇ° tkela ya tka. ma daŇ°ta, ma en…ôt ň§moe Ň°aqqile, ńć…ôńćkale, warde"
eng: Open your mouth, and raise your voice. Say what a pleasant place it is, this place. What a field, what springs and streams, sparrows and flowers.

trg: "la-zadet. qw…ôttox tkila b-…ôlha. xeret bas ň§qamox, la-xeret bara"
eng: "you shouldn't be afraid. Lean your strength in Gd. You shall look only on front of you, don't look back"

trg: b…ôrdan d…ôqna-xwara jim xd…ôre
eng: Suddenly, d…ôqna-xwara vanished

trg: kańćalona gal l…ôbba q…ôwya w…ôdle ma-t d…ôqna-xwara m…ôre baew
eng: Kańćalona, with a heavy heart, did what the d…ôqna-xwara told him.

trg: xzele b…ôrdan ya ńćol ya ň§byaban, ya x…ôŇ°k…ôt …ôlha kullu bh…ôru
eng: He saw suddenly that this desert this wilderness this darkness of Gd all brightened.

trg: Ň°…ômŇ°a ň§pl…ôtla, daŇ°ta kulla xd…ôra sq…ôlta: warde, ilane
eng: The sun came out, the field became all beautiful: flowers and trees.

trg: kańćalona p…ôre g-d-ayne daŇ°te ilane, ň§salmew idew aqlew x…ôllile
